title: Toad-in-the-hole
instructions: Cut out a circle from a piece of white bread.
Melt butter in a frying pan or griddle.
Put the bread in the pan.
Crack an egg and put it in the center hole of the bread.
Place the bread circle in the pan and toast on both sides.
Once lightly set, flip the bread over and cook on the other side.
notes: This was a quick meal specialty of Henry Solomon, who was a short order cook at a diner during the Great Depression and later became a restaurant manager and president of Foster's Diners.
A family favorite passed down from grandpa Henry.
